# gguf-guard

> Static analysis and integrity verification for GGUF model files

gguf-guard is a Go CLI tool that detects weight-level anomalies, structural tampering, and quantization-aware attacks in GGUF model files without running inference.

## Core Capabilities

- Parses GGUF v2/v3 binary format (header, metadata, tensor info, data)
- Dequantizes F32, F16, BF16, Q8_0, Q4_0, Q4_1 tensors to float32
- Computes per-tensor statistics: mean, variance, stddev, kurtosis, skewness, percentiles, outlier ratio
- Detects anomalies via four-layer scoring: tensor-local, role-group, model-global, reference
- Analyzes raw quantization blocks (Q4_0, Q4_1, Q4_K, Q5_K, Q6_K, Q8_0) for scale/code entropy, repeated blocks, saturation
- Uses robust statistics (median, MAD, trimmed mean, Tukey fences) to reduce false positives
- Generates per-tensor SHA-256 integrity manifests with Merkle tree root
- Validates structural policy (offsets, overlaps, metadata, tensor shapes)
- Matches model families (llama, mistral, mixtral, qwen2, gemma, phi) by tensor naming/shape patterns
- Compares quantized models against FP/BF16 source with per-format loss budgets

## Architecture

Three Go packages:

- `gguf/` — binary parser, type definitions, dequantization, block-level extraction
- `analysis/` — statistics, anomaly detection, robust stats, quant checks, reference profiles, manifests, policy, families, lineage, fingerprinting, comparison
- `cmd/gguf-guard/` — CLI with 10 subcommands (scan, fingerprint, compare, profile, build-reference, lineage, manifest, verify-manifest, inspect, info)

## Key Types

- `gguf.File` — parsed GGUF file (header, metadata, tensors, data offset)
- `gguf.TensorInfo` — name, dimensions, type, element count, offset
- `gguf.BlockStats` — block-level quantization statistics
- `analysis.TensorStats` — per-tensor statistical summary
- `analysis.AnomalyReport` — score, confidence, anomalies, layer scores
- `analysis.ReferenceProfile` — known-good tensor ranges with provenance
- `analysis.Manifest` — per-tensor SHA-256 hashes with Merkle root
- `analysis.PolicyReport` — structural validation pass/fail with violations
- `analysis.FamilyMatch` — architecture match with score
- `analysis.LineageResult` — source vs quantized comparison verdict

## Scoring

Score range 0.0 (clean) to 1.0 (suspicious). Layers: tensor-local (0.30), role-group (0.25), model-global (0.20), reference (0.25). Severity weights: critical +0.30, warning +0.10, info +0.02. Verdicts: PASS (<0.2), WARN (0.2-0.5), FAIL (>0.5).
